resolvedDesignBlockers.note: These three items were the specific blockers identified in the Claude amended-design re-review. Their resolution is a design-layer fix only; it does not change any topic's blockingStatus, does not approve any scientific correction, and does not authorize canonical generation.

instructorScriptFieldGaps.detail: mainIdea, intuition, graphGuidance, tableGuidance, levelAdaptations added to CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json (schemaVersion 2.1.0). See INSTRUCTOR_SCRIPT_FIELD_VALIDATION.md.

identifierRegistryConflictNamespace.detail: C01-C04 now match pattern ^C[0-9]{2}$ in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json (registryVersion 1.1.0).

periodFrequencyGlossaryTerms.detail: ch01-term-period and ch01-term-frequency added to BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json (glossaryVersion 1.1.0), linked to ch01-t03, SCA04, SCA05.

missingCorrectionRecordsForPilotTopics.detail: ch01-corr-006 (ch01-t03, SCA04/SCA05), ch01-corr-007 (ch01-t08, SCA12/S3-SCI-004), and ch01-corr-008 (ch01-t10, SCA11/S3-SCI-005) added to SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json (correctionLayerVersion 1.1.0), each linked to a new design-layer conflict record (CD-CONF-006/007/008 in DUPLICATE_AND_CONFLICT_DECISIONS.json, decisionLayerVersion 1.1.0). See scientificCorrectionApprovalForPilotTopics below for their current approval status.

scientificCorrectionApprovalForPilotTopi.detail: ch01-corr-006/007/008 now carry approvalStatus 'editoriallyApproved', with scientificReviewer/editorialReviewer populated as the project owner (khubrani2a-gif) and approvedAt 2026-07-15, on the basis of Claude's completed independent scientific review. 'scientificCorrection' was removed from ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10's blockingReason in this file accordingly.

pilotRightsPolicy.detail: PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json (approved 2026-07-15 by the project owner) commits ch01-t02/ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10 to fully original explanations and diagrams, excludes publisher-derived sources (P5 problems, R1 review cards) from pilot authoring, and declares the missing Source-002 visuals relevant to ch01-t02/ch01-t03 unnecessary for pilot purposes. 'rightsPending' removed from all four topics' blockingReason, and 'unverifiedVisual' removed from ch01-t02/ch01-t03's blockingReason, accordingly. This is a project content policy, not external legal rights clearance — see PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json's legalDisclaimer. canonicalGenerationAuthorized and studentFacingAllowed/studentPublicationAuthorized remain false throughout; this resolves listed blockers, it does not authorize generation. A requirement that every actual pilot visual be newly designed and scientifically reviewed is preserved in PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json's visualRequirement and in this file's pilotExitCriteria below.

canonicalGenerationAuthorizationNote: Chapter-wide authorization remains false because this authorization is scoped, not chapter-wide: only ch01-t02, ch01-t03, ch01-t08, and ch01-t10 are authorized for draft canonical pilot generation (see each topic's canonicalGenerationAuthorized below, and PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json). The other ten Chapter 1 topics remain unauthorized.

topics.title: Distance, Units, Area and Volume
topics.visualResolutionNote: ch01-vis-004's placeholders relevant to this topic (S2-SEG019/029/039) are deemed unnecessary for the pilot per PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json; retained here for traceability only, not as an open blocker.
topics.pilotScope: Structure and draft provenance plan only; use confirmed PDF visuals or approved original redraws, not unresolved Source 002 placeholders. All explanations and diagrams must be newly authored per PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json.

topics.title: Time, Period and Frequency
topics.visualResolutionNote: ch01-vis-004's placeholders relevant to this topic (S2-SEG019/049) are deemed unnecessary for the pilot per PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json; retained here for traceability only, not as an open blocker.
topics.pilotScope: Define one complete cycle and state the periodic-process condition before any student-facing wording is approved. All explanations and diagrams must be newly authored per PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json.

topics.title: Acceleration, Signs and g
topics.pilotScope: Declare the positive axis, separate the magnitude |g| from signed acceleration, and keep sign/direction validation explicit. All explanations and diagrams must be newly authored per PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json.

topics.title: Centripetal Acceleration
topics.pilotScope: State that velocity is tangent to the path while the velocity change and centripetal acceleration point inward. All explanations and diagrams must be newly authored per PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json.

batch1DraftingReadiness.note: This is a separate, narrower readiness state for Batch 1 (ch01-t01, ch01-t04) — English-language canonical DRAFTING, ENGLISH BASELINE APPROVAL (v1.6.0), GLOSSARY TERMINOLOGY RECONCILIATION (v1.7.0), ARABIC CANDIDATE-DRAFT GENERATION AUTHORIZATION (v1.8.0), a governance correction to that authorization's packaging model, terminology, and glossary-enforcement rule applied before any Arabic content was generated (v1.8.1), the explicit project-owner RATIFICATION of that corrected authorization (v1.8.2), explicit project-owner ARABIC BASELINE APPROVAL (baselineVersion 1.0.0) for both topics (v1.9.0), explicit project-owner VISUAL-PRODUCTION AUTHORIZATION for exactly two visual IDs (ch01-t01-visual-001, ch01-t04-visual-001) (v1.10.0), and, as of v1.11.0, explicit project-owner internal VISUAL APPROVAL of both produced visuals as Visual Batch 1 baseline version 1.0.0. It is not part of pilotTopicOrder or topics above, which remain scoped exactly to the four already build-authorized pilot topics and are unaffected by this section. canonicalGenerationAuthorized and studentPublicationAuthorized above remain chapter-wide false and are NOT changed by this section — this records a narrower, topic-scoped exception, governed by its own authorizationRecordId, not a chapter-wide readiness change. Neither English baseline approval, glossary approval, Arabic candidate-draft generation authorization (or its ratification), Arabic baseline approval, visual-production authorization, nor this internal visual approval is chapter-wide readiness, and none implies identifier-registration, application-integration authorization for these two topics, independent human scientific or visual review, or any authorization for any other Chapter 1 topic. Schema decision: rather than adding ch01-t01/ch01-t04 to the topics array above (whose schema implies application-build readiness tied to ch01-pilot-authorization-001, which does not apply here) or flipping the chapter-wide canonicalGenerationAuthorized flag, a new, self-contained, batch-scoped section was added instead.
batch1DraftingReadiness.authorizationSource: docs/content-design/chapter-01/PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json v1.3.2, batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization (corrected at v1.3.1, project-owner ratified at v1.3.2)

topics.title: Fundamental Quantities
topics.identifierRegistrationStatus: registered — as of registryVersion 1.3.0 (2026-07-17): instructorScript ch01-is-101 and all six ch01-t01-block-* contentBlock IDs are registered in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json's batch1IdentifierRegistration.entries; still required before any future application-integration step, not itself an application-integration authorization. Glossary term IDs (ch01-term-fundamental-quantity, ch01-term-derived-quantity, ch01-term-speed, ch01-term-scalar) remain glossary-local, governed exclusively inside BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json, and are deliberately not registered in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json, consistent with how all previously-approved terms were never separately registered there.

topics.title: Mass, Inertia and Weight

correctionApprovalQualification.ch01-corr-002: Approved with a recorded qualification: 'apparent weight' is scientifically valid, project-authored interpretive terminology, not verbatim source wording. See SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json ch01-corr-002.approvals.approvalBasis and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_CORRECTION_APPROVAL_RECORD.md.

topics.identifierRegistrationStatus: registered — as of registryVersion 1.3.0 (2026-07-17): instructorScript ch01-is-104, all six ch01-t04-block-* contentBlock IDs, and problem ch01-prob-104 are registered in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json's batch1IdentifierRegistration.entries; still required before any future application-integration step, not itself an application-integration authorization. Glossary term IDs (ch01-term-mass, ch01-term-weight, ch01-term-apparent-weight, ch01-term-acceleration) remain glossary-local, governed exclusively inside BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json, and are deliberately not registered in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json.
topics.authorizedOutputPath: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t04-content.json (file exists; English-baseline-approved as of ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0; file itself unmoved, unrenamed, unmodified by that approval)

doesNotAuthorize: Any modification of docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json or ch01-t04-content.json (englishBaselineFilesImmutable: true above) — the only authorized Arabic candidate output directory remains arabicCandidateOutputDirectory above

Any modification of the two approved Arabic candidate files themselves, or of arabic.translationStatus on any record within them — the approved checksums recorded in ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json establish what is approved; translationStatus remains "draft" on every record and is not changed to "approved" by baseline approval
Arabic canonical promotion beyond this internal Arabic baseline approval (this is a project-owner internal approval only, not independent human scientific approval)

Visual production actually having occurred (visualsProduced and each topic's visualProduced remain false above and on each topic — this section authorizes a future visual-production task; it does not itself perform production)
Tracing, copying, redrawing, or adapting any publisher slide, textbook page, or other cataloged source visual, even once visual production is undertaken
Any visual ID or topic other than ch01-t01-visual-001 and ch01-t04-visual-001
Independent human visual or scientific review of either visual (reviewer/reviewedAt must remain null on both validation records)
Application-route or topic-registry expansion in apps/chapter1-mvp/
Rendering ch01-t01 or ch01-t04 inside the PHSH111 application
Identifier registration in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json (see identifierRegistrationStatus on each topic above — pending, required before application integration, not before baseline or glossary approval)
Any change to canonicalGenerationAuthorized or studentPublicationAuthorized above, both of which remain chapter-wide false
batch1DraftingReadiness.englishBaselineApprovalNote: As of v1.6.0, English baseline approval HAS occurred for both ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 — see englishBaselineApproved, englishBaselineVersion, and englishBaselineApprovalRecordPath on this section and on each topic above, and docs/content-design/chapter-01/ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json for the full governance record. This is an English-content approval only; it does not authorize Arabic, visuals, application integration, or publication, all of which remain listed in doesNotAuthorize above. As of v1.8.1, these two approved files are additionally recorded as englishBaselineFilesImmutable: true — the Arabic candidate-draft generation authorized below must never edit them.
batch1DraftingReadiness.glossaryApprovalNote: As of v1.7.0, all Batch 1 blocking glossary terms are project-owner approved — see glossaryReconciliationComplete, glossaryActionsCompleted, and glossaryApprovalRecordPath above, and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_APPROVAL_RECORD.md for the full governance record. This is a project-owner terminology approval, not independent human scientific review, and did not itself authorize Arabic generation at v1.7.0 — that required a separate, future authorization decision, made at v1.8.0 (see arabicGenerationAuthorizationNote below).
batch1DraftingReadiness.arabicGenerationAuthorizationNote: As of v1.8.0, Arabic candidate-draft generation was authorized for both ch01-t01 and ch01-t04. Arabic candidate generation is authorized; Arabic canonical or baseline approval is not. See arabicGenerationAuthorized, arabicGenerationAuthorizedAt, arabicGenerationAuthorizationRecordPath, authorizationRecordId, and authorizationSource above, and docs/content-design/chapter-01/PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json v1.3.1 (batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization) plus docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md for the full governance record. As of v1.8.1, this authorization was corrected before any Arabic content was generated under it (see arabicGenerationCorrectionNote below). This authorization task did not itself generate any Arabic content — arabicContentGenerated and arabicCandidateFilesExist remain explicitly false above and on each topic. It does not authorize Arabic baseline approval, visual production, application integration, or publication, all of which remain listed in doesNotAuthorize above and require their own separate, future authorization decisions.
batch1DraftingReadiness.arabicGenerationCorrectionNote: As of v1.8.1, the v1.8.0 Arabic candidate-draft generation authorization was corrected — see arabicGenerationCorrectedAt, arabicGenerationCorrectionRecordPath, arabicGenerationModel, arabicCandidateOutputDirectory, and englishBaselineFilesImmutable above, and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_AUTHORIZATION_CORRECTION_RECORD.md for the full correction record. Three defects in the original authorization were fixed: (1) the packaging model changed from in-place editing of the approved English baseline files to separate immutable-original/candidate-copy files under docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/; (2) 'Arabic canonical generation' terminology was replaced with 'Arabic candidate-draft generation' throughout; (3) an absolute prohibition on terminology outside the nine Batch 1 glossary records was replaced with a rule enforcing those nine where applicable while permitting other approved glossary terms and ordinary Arabic language. No Arabic content had been generated before this correction was applied. This is a corrective patch, not a new grant of scope.
batch1DraftingReadiness.arabicGenerationRatificationNote: As of v1.8.2, the corrected Arabic candidate-draft generation authorization is explicitly ratified by the project owner — see arabicGenerationProjectOwnerRatified, arabicGenerationRatifiedAt, arabicGenerationRatifiedInAuthorizationVersion, and arabicGenerationRatificationRecordPath above, PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json v1.3.2 (batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization.projectOwnerRatification), and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_RATIFICATION_RECORD.md for the full record. Governance history recorded transparently: the original authorization (PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json v1.3.0) was created before the project owner had issued the intended explicit authorization decision; v1.3.1 technically corrected its packaging/terminology/glossary defects but was a correction task, not the explicit ratification itself; v1.3.2 supplies the explicit project-owner ratification of the already-corrected scope. As of v1.9.0, arabicContentGenerated, arabicCandidateFilesExist, and arabicCandidateDirectoryExists are all true (see arabicBaselineApprovalNote below) — at v1.8.2 itself they were still false, since the candidate-generation task had not yet run.
batch1DraftingReadiness.arabicBaselineApprovalNote: As of v1.9.0, the project owner has explicitly approved both Arabic candidate drafts as Arabic Batch 1 baseline version 1.0.0 — see arabicContentGenerated, arabicDraftReviewComplete, arabicBaselineApproved, arabicBaselineVersion, arabicBaselineApprovedAt, arabicBaselineApprovalRecordPath, and arabicBaselineApprovalDocPath above and on each topic, and docs/content-design/chapter-01/ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json (the external governance record; a new, separate record, not merged into ARABIC_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json) plus docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_BASELINE_APPROVAL_RECORD.md for full detail. This rests on docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_DRAFT_GENERATION_REPORT.md (generation, zero English-content drift) and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_DRAFT_REVIEW_AND_REVISION_REPORT.md (consolidated review, two narrow defects corrected, 'Remaining blockers: None', 'Ready for Arabic baseline-approval decision' for both topics). This is a project-owner internal Arabic baseline approval only — it is explicitly NOT independent human scientific approval (independentHumanReviewComplete remains false), NOT visual approval (visualProductionAuthorized remains false), NOT application-integration authorization (applicationIntegrationAuthorized and applicationExpansionAuthorized remain false), and NOT student-facing or publication authorization (studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false everywhere). Neither Arabic candidate file was moved, copied, renamed, or internally rewritten by this approval, and no record's arabic.translationStatus was changed to "approved" — it remains "draft" everywhere; the external ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json record is what establishes approval status, exactly mirroring the pattern already used for the English Batch 1 baseline and the four-topic pilot baselines. identifierRegistrationStatus remains pending on each topic, unchanged by this approval.
batch1DraftingReadiness.visualProductionAuthorizationNote: As of v1.10.0, the project owner has explicitly authorized original, rights-safe visual PRODUCTION for exactly two visual IDs — ch01-t01-visual-001 and ch01-t04-visual-001 — see visualProductionAuthorized, visualProductionAuthorizedAt, visualProductionAuthorizationRecordPath, authorizedVisualIds, and visualOutputDirectory above and on each topic, and docs/content-design/chapter-01/PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json v1.4.0 (batch1VisualProductionAuthorization) plus docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_VISUAL_PRODUCTION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md for full detail. This note supersedes, for current-state purposes, the 'NOT visual approval (visualProductionAuthorized remains false)' clause in arabicBaselineApprovalNote above, which remains accurate as a historical record of the state at v1.9.0 and is not rewritten. This authorization task did not itself produce any visual — visualsProduced and each topic's visualProduced remained explicitly false at v1.10.0; visualDirectoryExists remained false; docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-visuals/ did not yet exist. This historical record is preserved and not rewritten; see visualApprovalNote below for the current state as of v1.11.0.
batch1DraftingReadiness.visualApprovalNote: As of v1.11.0, both visuals have been produced (a separate, subsequent task, not itself authorized by v1.10.0's authorization-only grant), consolidated-reviewed with two narrow ch01-t01 corrections applied and no ch01-t04 defect found (docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_VISUAL_REVIEW_AND_REVISION_REPORT.md, 'Remaining blockers: None' for both), and the project owner has now explicitly approved both, at the exact reviewed SVG checksums, as Visual Batch 1 baseline version 1.0.0 — see visualDirectoryExists, visualsProduced, visualReviewComplete, visualReviewRecordPath, visualApproved, visualApprovedAt, visualBaselineVersion, visualApprovalRecordPath, visualApprovalDocPath, and approvedVisualChecksums above, and on each topic, plus docs/content-design/chapter-01/VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json (the external governance record) and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_VISUAL_APPROVAL_RECORD.md for full detail. This supersedes, for current-state purposes, the 'visualsProduced and each topic's visualProduced remain explicitly false' and 'visualDirectoryExists remains false' clauses in visualProductionAuthorizationNote above, both of which remain accurate as a historical record of the state at v1.10.0 and are not rewritten. This is a project-owner internal visual-baseline approval only — it is explicitly NOT independent human visual or scientific approval (independentHumanReviewComplete remains false), NOT identifier registration (identifierRegistrationComplete remains false), NOT application-integration authorization (applicationIntegrationAuthorized and applicationExpansionAuthorized remain false), and NOT student-facing or publication authorization (studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false everywhere). Neither SVG nor either validation record was moved, renamed, or rewritten by this approval, and neither record's reviewer/reviewedAt fields were changed from null — the external VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json record is what establishes approval status, exactly mirroring the pattern already used for the English and Arabic Batch 1 baselines. VISUAL_HOUSE_STYLE.md was not modified by this approval; the brick-red force color used in ch01-t04-visual-001 remains a provisional candidate for these two assets only, not a ratified chapter-wide palette extension.
batch1DraftingReadiness.identifierRegistrationNote: As of v1.12.0, the project owner has registered exactly the 18 Batch 1 content, problem, source-marker, and approved-visual identifiers required before a future application-integration step — see identifierRegistrationComplete, identifierRegistrationCompletedAt, identifierRegistryVersion, identifierRegistrationRecordPath, totalIdentifiersRegistered, identifierRegistrationBreakdown, and glossaryIdsRemainGlossaryLocal above, and docs/content-design/chapter-01/IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json (now registryVersion 1.3.0, batch1IdentifierRegistration) plus docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_IDENTIFIER_REGISTRATION_RECORD.md for full detail. This supersedes, for current-state purposes, the 'NOT identifier registration (identifierRegistrationComplete remains false)' clause in visualApprovalNote above, which remains accurate as a historical record of the state at v1.11.0 and is not rewritten. This is an identifier-registration and governance-synchronization action only — it is explicitly NOT independent human visual or scientific approval (independentHumanReviewComplete remains false), NOT application-integration authorization (applicationIntegrationAuthorized and applicationExpansionAuthorized remain false), and NOT student-facing or publication authorization (studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false everywhere). No approved English/Arabic topic file, SVG, or visual-validation record was modified by this registration. Glossary term IDs (the nine Batch 1 terms) remain governed exclusively inside BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json, glossaryVersion 1.3.0, unchanged, and were deliberately NOT registered as entries in IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json.
batch1DraftingReadiness.applicationIntegrationAuthorizationNote: As of v1.13.0, the project owner has explicitly authorized INTERNAL APPLICATION INTEGRATION (not student-facing release, not deployment, not publication) for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 — see applicationIntegrationAuthorized, applicationIntegrationAuthorizedAt, applicationIntegrationAuthorizationRecordPath, applicationIntegrationImplemented, and integrationSourceModel above, and each topic's own applicationExpansionAuthorized/applicationExpansionImplemented fields, plus docs/content-design/chapter-01/PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json v1.5.0 (batch1ApplicationIntegrationAuthorization) and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_APPLICATION_INTEGRATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md for full detail. This supersedes, for current-state purposes, the 'NOT application-integration authorization (applicationIntegrationAuthorized and applicationExpansionAuthorized remain false)' clauses in arabicBaselineApprovalNote, visualApprovalNote, and identifierRegistrationNote above, all of which remain accurate as historical records of their respective states and are not rewritten. The selected integration-source model is direct import from the six approved source paths (English, Arabic, visuals) with an in-memory English/Arabic merge step, mirroring the existing rawImports.ts pattern used by the four pilot topics — no duplicate editable source of truth. **This authorization task did not itself perform any integration** — applicationIntegrationImplemented and each topic's applicationExpansionImplemented remain explicitly false; no route, import, registry entry, test, or build-configuration change was made. This is an authorization and architecture-decision action only — it is explicitly NOT independent human visual or scientific review (independentHumanReviewComplete remains false), NOT student-facing or publication authorization (studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false everywhere), and does not itself constitute or imply QA having occurred. No approved English/Arabic topic file, SVG, visual-validation record, or application file was modified by this authorization.
batch1DraftingReadiness.applicationIntegrationVerificationNote: As of v1.14.0, the authorized application integration has been implemented and verified in an environment with Node.js/npm available: TypeScript typecheck passes clean, the full test suite passes (131/131 tests across 9 files, including 4 new Batch 1 test files), the production build succeeds (81 modules), and local browser QA confirmed all six topics render in the correct order (ch01-t01, ch01-t02, ch01-t03, ch01-t04, ch01-t08, ch01-t10), both new topics render correctly in English and Arabic, ch01-t01 shows no empty problem/solution UI, ch01-t04 renders ch01-prob-104 with 441 N as the labeled unrounded intermediate and 4.4 x 10^2 N as the final result, Review Card labels and instructor-only visibility are correct, the visual dialogs (enlarge/Escape/close/focus-trap/scroll-lock) work for both new visuals, mobile and desktop layouts are usable, and the four original pilot topics show no regression. Two narrow test-only defects (an HTML-entity-escaping assertion mismatch in two new test files, and a static-markup assertion that didn't account for the instructor panel's collapsed-by-default state) were found and fixed during verification — both fixes were in the test files this project's own prior task created, not in any approved content or product code; see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_APPLICATION_VERIFICATION_AND_QA_REPORT.md for full detail. This supersedes, for current-state purposes, the 'This authorization task did not itself perform any integration' clause in applicationIntegrationAuthorizationNote above, which remains an accurate historical record of the state at v1.13.0 and is not rewritten. independentHumanReviewComplete, studentFacingAllowed, and studentPublicationAuthorized all remain false — this is internal verification only, not independent human review, not student-facing authorization, and not publication authorization. No public deployment occurred.

pilotExitCriteria: Claude approves the amended design layer
The user explicitly authorizes pilot canonical generation
Every pilot block validates against CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json
Every source locator validates against IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json
Linked scientific corrections are approved or the block remains instructor/internal only

Rights and visual blockers are resolved for student-facing blocks (for ch01-t02/ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10, resolved via PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json's original-content-only commitment, not external legal clearance)

Every visual actually used in ch01-t02/ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10 is newly designed and passes scientific review before use — no publisher visual may be traced, cropped, or reused, per PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json
English/Arabic/Bilingual status is explicit without generating missing Arabic
Student and instructor visibility passes content-leak testing
